release_review.summary: Desktop-managed SSH Host sessions now expose Remote runtime and Desktop model sources as explicit user-selectable choices while keeping Desktop model-source binding failures non-fatal for SSH startup.
release_review.same_window_rationale: This release changes Desktop and Env App source-selection behavior around an existing runtime-service capability and binding payload, but it does not add a new runtime protocol version, compatibility epoch, minimum Desktop version, or minimum Runtime version. The unchanged window stays safe because older compatible runtimes already report the desktop_ai_broker capability and binding state, while unavailable or failed Desktop source binding now degrades to source availability instead of changing startup or wire-contract requirements.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service snapshot capability and binding state normalization
Desktop SSH Host startup progress and stop-opening operation semantics
Desktop AI Broker startup report model availability and configured-state propagation
Desktop AI Broker reverse-forward failure handling during SSH bootstrap
Desktop AI Broker runtime-control binding failure handling after Local UI forward verification
Env App Remote runtime and Desktop model-source grouping and selection
Env App default model and active thread model source switching
Env App Runtime Settings Desktop model-source status and binding-state rendering
SSH-managed runtime bootstrap, Local UI health, and startup report refresh paths
